components.name: App bar
components.description:
Use a top app bar to provide content and actions related to a current screen, such as navigation, screen headlines, and actions.


Focus on describing the current page and provide 1–2 essential actions.
Displays labels and page navigation controls at the top of the page. (Use a toolbar to display page actions)
Three types: small, medium flexible, large flexible
On scroll, apply a fill color to separate from body content
Can animate on and off screen with another bar of controls, like a row of chips


Guidelines

components.name: Assistive chip
components.description: Assist chips represent smart or automated actions that can span multiple apps. They should appear dynamically and contextually in a UI.

components.name: Badges
components.description: Badges are used to indicate a notification, item count, or other information relating to a navigation destination. They are placed on the ending edge of icons, typically within other components.

components.name: Basic dialog
components.description:
A Dialog is a modal window that appears in front of app content to provide critical information or ask for a decision. They disable all app functionality when they appear, and remain on screen until a required action has been taken.

Being purposefully interruptive they should be used sparingly. To provide options without interrupting a user’s experience use a Menu.

components.name: Bottom app bar
components.description:
Bottom app bars provide access to up to four actions, including the floating action button (FAB). Bottom app bars should be used for mobile devices only
🛠 Additional optionsIcon buttons - Change Icon buttons by selecting the individual icon buttons and editing the variant properties for states and icon values.
FAB - Change the FAB by selecting the FAB and editing the variant properties for states.

components.name: Bottom sheet
components.description:
Bottom sheets are surfaces containing supplementary content, anchored to the bottom of the screen.


🛠 Additional options
Drag handle - can be toggled to hide or show

components.name: Button
components.description:
Buttons communicate actions that users can take.


Can contain an optional leading icon
Five color options: elevated, filled, tonal, outlined, and text
Five size recommendations: extra small, small, medium, large, extra large
Two shape options: round and square
Keep labels concise and use sentence-case

components.name: Button - elevated
components.description:
Buttons communicate actions that users can take.


Can contain an optional leading icon
Five color options: elevated, filled, tonal, outlined, and text
Five size recommendations: extra small, small, medium, large, extra large
Two shape options: round and square
Keep labels concise and use sentence-case

components.name: Button - outline
components.description:
Buttons communicate actions that users can take.


Can contain an optional leading icon
Five color options: elevated, filled, tonal, outlined, and text
Five size recommendations: extra small, small, medium, large, extra large
Two shape options: round and square
Keep labels concise and use sentence-case

components.name: Button - text
components.description:
Buttons communicate actions that users can take.


Can contain an optional leading icon
Five color options: elevated, filled, tonal, outlined, and text
Five size recommendations: extra small, small, medium, large, extra large
Two shape options: round and square
Keep labels concise and use sentence-case

components.name: Button - tonal
components.description:
Buttons communicate actions that users can take.


Can contain an optional leading icon
Five color options: elevated, filled, tonal, outlined, and text
Five size recommendations: extra small, small, medium, large, extra large
Two shape options: round and square
Keep labels concise and use sentence-case

components.name: Carousel
components.description:
A Carousel contains a collection of items that can be scrolled on and off the screen.

🛠️
Select between Uniform and Non-uniform layouts;
Choose Mobile or Tablet context
An optional item-text’s visibility can be toggled on and off

components.name: Carousel - Full screen
components.description:
A Carousel contains a collection of items that can be scrolled on and off the screen.

🛠️
Select between Uniform and Non-uniform layouts;
Choose Mobile or Tablet context
An optional item-text’s visibility can be toggled on and off

components.name: Centered slider
components.description: Centered sliders have the starting value in the middle of the slider.

components.name: Checkboxes
components.description: Checkboxes are a selection control which allow users to select one or more options from a set; present a list containing sub-selections; and turn an item on or off in a desktop environment

components.name: Circular-determinate progress indicator
components.description:
Determinate indicators display how long a process will take. They should be used when the process completion rate can be detected.


Use the same configuration for all instances of a process (like loading)
They capture attention through motion
Option to apply a wave to the active track for use case which would benefit from increased expressiveness

components.name: Circular-indeterminate progress indicator
components.description:
Indeterminate indicators express an unspecified amount of wait time. They should be used when progress isn’t detectable, or if it’s not necessary to indicate how long an activity will take.


Use the same configuration for all instances of a process (like loading)
They capture attention through motion
Option to apply a wave to the active track for use case which would benefit from increased expressiveness

components.name: Connected button group
components.description:
Connected button groups help people select options, switch views, or sort elements in a page. They behave similarly to standard groups, except they don’t affect adjacent buttons.


Applies shape morph when pressed or selected
Connected button groups replace the segmented button
Works with all button sizes: XS, S, M, L, and XL
Support for single-select, multi-select, and selection-required

components.name: Dial picker
components.description:
Time pickers help users select and set a specific time.

🛠 Additional options
Time picker input - Edit the selection of the input by selecting the individual input and editing the variant properties.

Period selector - Change between AM or PM as well as states by selecting the selector and editing the variant properties.

Hour Line - Change angle of hour and time highlighted by selecting the hour line and editing the variant property.

components.name: Docked input date picker [desktop]
components.description:
Date pickers let users select a date, or a range of dates.

🛠 Additional options 
Days/Years - edit the states of the days/years by selecting the individual day/year and editing the variant properties.

Menu button - edit menu button states by selecting and editing variant properties.

components.name: Extended FAB
components.description:
Extended floating action buttons (extended FABs) help people take primary actions.


Use for the most common or important action on a screen
Three sizes: small, medium, large
Use instead of FAB when label text is needed to understand action

components.name: FAB
components.description:
Floating action buttons (FABs) help people take primary actions on a screen. The FAB appears in front of all other content on screen, and is recognizable for its rounded shape and icon in the center. Medium FABs are recommended to use as the default FAB site.


Use a FAB for the most common or important action on a screen
Make sure the icon in a FAB is clear and understandable
FABs persist on the screen when content is scrolling

components.name: FAB menu
components.description:
The FAB menu opens from a FAB to display multiple related actions


Opens from a FAB to show 2–6 related actions floating on screen
One FAB menu size for all sizes of FABs
Not used with extended FABs

components.name: fast_forward_filled
components.description:
Fast Forward, Skip Ahead, Later
Use for: Quickly advancing in media

components.name: fast_rewind_filled
components.description:
Rewind, Previous, Backtrack
Use for: Quickly going back in media

components.name: Filter chip
components.description:
Filter chips use tags or descriptive words to filter content. They can be a good alternative to toggle buttons or checkboxes.

Tapping on a filter chip activates it and appends a leading checkmark icon to the starting edge of the chip label.

components.name: Horizontal card
components.description: Cards are used to display content and actions on a single topic. They should be easy to scan for relevant and actionable information. Cards can be displayed together in a grid, vertical list, or carousel.

components.name: Icon button
components.description:
Default icon buttons can open other elements, like a menu or search.


Icon buttons must use a system icon with a clear meaning
Many configurations: Color, size, width, and shape
On web, display a tooltip describing the action when hovering

components.name: Icon button togglable
components.description:
Toggle icon buttons can represent binary actions that can be toggled on and off, such as favorite or bookmark.


Icon buttons must use a system icon with a clear meaning
Many configurations: Color, size, width, and shape
On web, display a tooltip describing the action when hovering
In toggle buttons, use the outlined style of an icon for the unselected state, and the filled style for the selected state

components.name: inbox
components.description: archive, email, inbox, incoming, mail, message

components.name: Input chip
components.description: Input chips represent discrete pieces of information entered by a user, such as Gmail contacts or filter options within a search field.

components.name: Input date picker
components.description:
Date pickers let users select a date, or a range of dates.

🛠 Additional options 
Fields - edit the states of the fields by selecting the individual field and editing the variant properties.

components.name: Keyboard
components.description:
Use this Keyboard Component in your prototypes and wireframes. (This component is for placement only and is not an accurate representation of Android’s Gboard)

🛠
Toggle between different Configurations (Base keyboard, Alphanumeric, Keypad, Numeric only ) and Styles (Portrait, Landscape, Floating)

components.name: Keyboard picker
components.description:
Time pickers help users select and set a specific time.

🛠 Additional options
Time picker input - You can edit the selection of the input by selecting the individual input and editing the variant properties.

Period selector - You can change between AM or PM as well as states by selecting the selector and editing the variant properties.

components.name: Linear-determinate progress indicator
components.description:
Determinate indicators display how long a process will take. They should be used when the process completion rate can be detected.


Use the same configuration for all instances of a process (like loading)
They capture attention through motion
Option to apply a wave to the active track for use case which would benefit from increased expressiveness

components.name: Linear-indeterminate progress indicator
components.description:
Indeterminate indicators express an unspecified amount of wait time. They should be used when progress isn’t detectable, or if it’s not necessary to indicate how long an activity will take.


Use the same configuration for all instances of a process (like loading)
They capture attention through motion
Option to apply a wave to the active track for use case which would benefit from increased expressiveness

components.name: list
components.description:
List, Items, Inventory, Collection, Series
Use for: Displaying a list of items or options.

components.name: List (baseline)
components.description:
Lists are vertically organized groups of text and images (List Items).

Density can be used in denser UIs where space is limited. Density is only applied to the height.

🛠 Additional options 
List item - can be edited by selecting an individual list item and  selecting states, leading icons, trailing icons, and dividers from the variant properties.

components.name: List dialog
components.description:
A Dialog is a modal window that appears in front of app content to provide critical information or ask for a decision. They disable all app functionality when they appear, and remain on screen until a required action has been taken.

Being purposefully interruptive they should be used sparingly. To provide options without interrupting a user’s experience use a Menu.

🛠 Additional options 
List item - can be edited by selecting an individual list item and  selecting states, leading icons, trailing icons, and dividers from the variant properties.

components.name: List: -2 Density (baseline)
components.description:
Lists are vertically organized groups of text and images (List Items).

Density can be used in denser UIs where space is limited. Density is only applied to the height.

🛠 Additional options 
List item - can be edited by selecting an individual list item and  selecting states, leading icons, trailing icons, and dividers from the variant properties.

components.name: List: -4 Density (baseline)
components.description:
Lists are vertically organized groups of text and images (List Items).

Density can be used in denser UIs where space is limited. Density is only applied to the height.

🛠 Additional options 
List item - can be edited by selecting an individual list item and  selecting states, leading icons, trailing icons, and dividers from the variant properties.

components.name: Loading indicator
components.description:
Loading indicators show progress for a short wait time and are recommended as a replacement for indeterminate circular progress indicators.


Always reflect an ongoing process and are never simply decorative
Used for pull-to-refresh interactions
Not used for processes that transition from indeterminate to determinate
Capture attention through motion

components.name: Menu - Icon button Example
components.description:
This is an example of the Menu in use. Menus display a list of choices on a temporary surface. They appear when users interact with a button, action, or other control.  

🛠 Additional options 
Menu list item - can be edited by selecting individual list item and  selecting wether it is enabled, selected, and has a divider from the variant properties.

components.name: Menu (baseline)
components.description:
Menus display a list of choices on a temporary surface. They appear when users interact with a button, action, or other control.
🛠 Additional optionsMenu list item - can be edited by selecting individual list item and selecting wether it is enabled, selected, and has a divider from the variant properties.

components.name: Menu with Text field - Example 1
components.description:
This is an example of the Menu in use. Menus display a list of choices on a temporary surface. They appear when users interact with a button, action, or other control.  

🛠 Additional options 
Menu list item - can be edited by selecting individual list item and  selecting wether it is enabled, selected, and has a divider from the variant properties.

components.name: Menu with Text field - Example 2
components.description:
This is an example of the Menu in use. Menus display a list of choices on a temporary surface. They appear when users interact with a button, action, or other control.  

🛠 Additional options 
Menu list item - can be edited by selecting individual list item and  selecting wether it is enabled, selected, and has a divider from the variant properties.

components.name: Modal date picker
components.description:
Date pickers let users select a date, or a range of dates.

🛠 Additional options 
Days/Years - edit the states of the days/years by selecting the individual day/year and editing the variant properties.

Menu button - edit menu button states by selecting and editing variant properties.

components.name: Navigation Bar: Horizontal items
components.description:
Navigation bars (nav bars) provide access to three to six destinations. This design is specifically for the Medium Window Size Class (Portrait Tablet and Portrait Foldable).


The nav bar is positioned at the bottom of screens for convenient access. Each destination is represented by an icon and optional text label.
When a navigation bar icon is tapped or focused, the user is taken to the navigation destination associated with that icon.


🛠 Additional options
Segments - can be edited by selecting individual segments and selecting states, labels, and badges from the variant properties.

components.name: Navigation Bar: Vertical items
components.description:
Navigation bars (nav bars) provide access to three to five destinations. This design is specifically for the Compact Window Size Class (Mobile Portrait).


The nav bar is positioned at the bottom of screens for convenient access. Each destination is represented by an icon and optional text label.
When a navigation bar icon is tapped or focused, the user is taken to the navigation destination associated with that icon.




🛠 Additional options
Segments - can be edited by selecting individual segments and selecting states, labels, and badges from the variant properties.

components.name: Navigation Drawer
components.description:
Navigation drawers provide access to destinations and app functionality, such as switching accounts. They can either be permanently on-screen or opened and closed by a navigation menu icon.

Navigation drawers are recommended for apps with 5 or more top-level destinations, apps with 2 or more levels of navigation hierarchy, quick navigation between unrelated destinations, or replacing the navigation rail or navigation bar on large screens.

🛠 Additional options 
Nav Item - Can be edited by selecting individual item and  selecting style, states, icons, and metadata from the variant properties.

components.name: Navigation Rail
components.description:
The rail is a side navigation component that displays three to seven app destinations and, optionally, a floating action button (FAB). Each destination is represented by an icon and label text.


The rail can serve as the sole navigation component on larger screen sizes, such as desktops and tablets.


🛠 Additional options
Segments - Can be edited by selecting individual segments and selecting states, labels, and badges from the variant properties.

components.name: Navigation Rail: Expanded
components.description:
Expanded navigation rails provide access to destinations and app functionality, such as switching accounts. They can either be permanently on-screen or opened and closed by a navigation menu icon.


Expanded Navigation rails are recommended for apps with 5 or more top-level destinations, apps with 2 or more levels of navigation hierarchy, quick navigation between unrelated destinations, or replacing the navigation rail or navigation bar on large screens.


🛠 Additional options
Nav Item - Can be edited by selecting individual item and selecting style, states, icons, and metadata from the variant properties.

components.name: pause
components.description:
Pause, Stop, Break, Interrupt, Suspend
Use for: Temporarily stopping or pausing an activity

components.name: Plain Tooltip
components.description: Plain tooltips briefly describe a UI element. They're best used for labelling UI elements with no text, like icon-only buttons and fields.

components.name: play_arrow_filled
components.description:
Play, Start, Begin
Use for: Starting or resuming playback

components.name: Radio buttons
components.description: Radio buttons are the recommended way to allow users to make a single selection from a list of options.

components.name: Range slider
components.description: Range selection sliders have two handles that set the minimum and maximum of a range.

components.name: Rich Tooltip
components.description:
Rich tooltips provide additional context about a UI element. They can optionally contain a subhead, buttons, and hyperlinks.
Rich tooltips are best used for longer text like definitions or explanations.

components.name: Scrollable list dialog
components.description:
A Dialog is a modal window that appears in front of app content to provide critical information or ask for a decision. They disable all app functionality when they appear, and remain on screen until a required action has been taken.

Most dialog content should avoid scrolling. Even when scrolling is required, the dialog title is pinned at the top, with buttons pinned at the bottom. Dialogs don’t scroll with elements outside of the dialog, such as the background.

Being purposefully interruptive they should be used sparingly. To provide options without interrupting a user’s experience use a Menu.

🛠 Additional options 
List item - can be edited by selecting an individual list item and  selecting states, leading icons, trailing icons, and dividers from the variant properties.

components.name: Search bar
components.description: Search allows users to enter a keyword or phrase and get relevant information. It’s an alternative to other forms of navigation.

components.name: Search docked layout
components.description:
Search allows users to enter a keyword or phrase and get relevant information. It’s an alternative to other forms of navigation.
––
Additional options
List items - Change list items by selecting the individual list item and editing the variant properties.

components.name: Search docked layout (baseline)
components.description:
Search allows users to enter a keyword or phrase and get relevant information. It’s an alternative to other forms of navigation.
––
Additional options 
List items - Change list items by selecting the individual list item and editing the variant properties.

components.name: Search full-screen layout
components.description:
Search allows users to enter a keyword or phrase and get relevant information. It’s an alternative to other forms of navigation.
––
Additional options
List items - Change list items by selecting the individual list item and editing the variant properties.

components.name: Search full-screen layout (baseline)
components.description:
Search allows users to enter a keyword or phrase and get relevant information. It’s an alternative to other forms of navigation.
––
Additional options 
List items - Change list items by selecting the individual list item and editing the variant properties.

components.name: Segmented button
components.description:
Segmented buttons help people select options, switch views or sort elements. 

🛠 Additional options
Button segment - edit the states, style and icon options of button segments by selecting the individual segment and editing the variant properties.

components.name: Side Sheet
components.description:
Side sheets are surfaces containing supplementary content or actions to support tasks as part of a flow. They are typically anchored on the right edge of larger screens for tablets and desktops. 

There are two types of side sheets: standard and modal.

components.name: skip_next_filled
components.description:
Next, Skip forward, Following
Use for: Skipping to the next track

components.name: skip_previous_filled
components.description:
Previous, Skip back, Earlier
Use for: Skipping to the previous track

components.name: Snackbar
components.description: Snackbars inform users of a process that an app has performed or will perform. They appear temporarily, towards the bottom of the screen. They shouldn’t interrupt the user experience, and they don’t require user input to disappear.

components.name: Split button
components.description:
Split buttons are used to add a menu of actions alongside a main action. This reduces visual complexity by hiding extra options. Split buttons work well alone or alongside common buttons and icon buttons.


Use to show an action with a menu of related actions
Same size range as buttons and icon buttons: XS, S, M, L, XL

components.name: Stacked card
components.description: Cards are used to display content and actions on a single topic. They should be easy to scan for relevant and actionable information. Cards can be displayed together in a grid, vertical list, or carousel.

components.name: Standard button group
components.description:
Standard button groups add interaction between adjacent buttons so they respond to each other.


Applies shape morph when pressed or selected
Works with all button sizes: XS, S, M, L, and XL
Support for single-select, multi-select, and selection-required

components.name: Standard slider
components.description: Standard sliders can be freely dragged to any value in the range. A slider can be either continuous or discrete.

components.name: Suggestion chip
components.description: Suggestion chips help narrow a user’s intent by presenting dynamically generated suggestions

components.name: Switch
components.description: Switches are the preferred way to adjust settings. They're used to control binary options – think On/Off or True/False.

components.name: Tabs
components.description:
Tabs organize and allow navigation between groups of content that are related and at the same level of hierarchy.


🛠 Additional options
Tab - Select the state of tabs by selecting the individual tab and editing the variant properties.

components.name: Text field
components.description:
Use a text field when someone needs to enter text into a UI, such as filling in contact or payment information.

🛠 Additional options
Support text - you can toggle supporting text between hide and show.

components.name: Toggle button
components.description:
Toggle buttons should be used for binary selections, like Save or Favorite. When toggle buttons are pressed, they can change color, shape, and labels.


Toggle buttons should use an outlined icon when unselected, and a filled version of the icon when selected. If a filled version doesn’t exist, increase the weight instead.


Can contain an optional leading icon
Four color options: elevated, filled, tonal, and outlined
Five size recommendations: extra small, small, medium, large, extra large
Two shape options: round and square
Keep labels concise and use sentence-case

components.name: Toggle button - elevated
components.description:
Toggle buttons should be used for binary selections, like Save or Favorite. When toggle buttons are pressed, they can change color, shape, and labels.


Toggle buttons should use an outlined icon when unselected, and a filled version of the icon when selected. If a filled version doesn’t exist, increase the weight instead.


Can contain an optional leading icon
Four color options: elevated, filled, tonal, and outlined
Five size recommendations: extra small, small, medium, large, extra large
Two shape options: round and square
Keep labels concise and use sentence-case

components.name: Toggle button - outline
components.description:
Toggle buttons should be used for binary selections, like Save or Favorite. When toggle buttons are pressed, they can change color, shape, and labels.


Toggle buttons should use an outlined icon when unselected, and a filled version of the icon when selected. If a filled version doesn’t exist, increase the weight instead.


Can contain an optional leading icon
Four color options: elevated, filled, tonal, and outlined
Five size recommendations: extra small, small, medium, large, extra large
Two shape options: round and square
Keep labels concise and use sentence-case

components.name: Toggle button - tonal
components.description:
Toggle buttons should be used for binary selections, like Save or Favorite. When toggle buttons are pressed, they can change color, shape, and labels.


Toggle buttons should use an outlined icon when unselected, and a filled version of the icon when selected. If a filled version doesn’t exist, increase the weight instead.


Can contain an optional leading icon
Four color options: elevated, filled, tonal, and outlined
Five size recommendations: extra small, small, medium, large, extra large
Two shape options: round and square
Keep labels concise and use sentence-case

components.name: Toolbar
components.description:
Use a toolbar to provide actions related to the current page. Toolbars can contain many actions and can scale to show more actions in larger windows.


Two types: docked (formerly bottom app bar) and floating
Use the vibrant color style for greater emphasis
Can display a wide variety of control types, like buttons, icon buttons, and text fields
Can be paired with FABs to emphasize certain actions
Don’t show at the same time as a navigation bar
